title: Telegraph: gt telegraph start cobra subcommand + L1→L2→L3 wiring + tests
description:
attached_molecule: gt-wisp-12qb
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-04-26T13:13:09Z
attached_vars: ["base_branch=main","test_command=go test ./...","merge_strategy=pr","require_review=true"]
dispatched_by: mayor
merge_strategy: mr
formula_vars: base_branch=main
test_command=go test ./...
merge_strategy=pr
require_review=true

Phase 1 of epic gt-mwy. Add gt telegraph start cobra subcommand that constructs and runs the full Telegraph pipeline for a single town, mirroring the wiring in internal/telegraph/integration_test.go:78-123 with production substitutions.

== CLI surface ==
- gt telegraph (new parent command, GroupID: GroupAgents) with subcommands.
- gt telegraph start --help documents:
    --config     (default: <townRoot>/settings/telegraph.toml)
    --town-root  (default: cwd-resolved via workspace.FindFromCwdOrError)
    Long help documents exit codes: 0 clean shutdown; 1 infra/config error.
- New file: internal/cmd/telegraph.go (and helpers as needed).

== Behavior (acceptance) ==
- Missing config file: exit non-zero, message wraps 'telegraph config not found: <path>' (already produced by telegraph.LoadWithDefaults).
- Invalid config (e.g. enabled=true with no secret_env): exit non-zero with the validate error from Config.Validate() (internal/telegraph/config.go:107-137).
- Enabled provider but unset env var: exit non-zero with 'telegraph: provider \"jira\": env var \"GT_TELEGRAPH_JIRA_SECRET\" is unset'. NOTE: ProviderConfig.ResolveSecret already returns 'env var X is unset or empty'; the wiring layer is responsible for prepending 'telegraph: provider \"<id>\":'.
- Valid config + secret: bind cfg.Telegraph.ListenAddr, log one-line '[Telegraph] listening on :8765, providers=[jira]' startup line, stay running.
- SIGINT/SIGTERM: close listener, drain rawCh, wait for dispatch goroutine to finish, emit '[Telegraph] shutdown complete', exit 0.
- Port already bound: exit non-zero with the listen error wrapped (do NOT swallow EADDRINUSE).

== Wiring fidelity ==
Mirror integration_test.go:78-123 with these production substitutions:
- transform.NewProduction(townRoot, cfg.BodyCap, parsedNudgeWindow, tlogger) instead of in-memory transform.New(memRouter, captureNudger, ...).
- tlog.Logger constructed from cfg.LogFile (or os.Stderr when empty).
- Single dispatch goroutine, range-over-channel; on translate error → increment a drop counter and log; on transform error → log + drop.
- Translator registry built only from providers with Enabled=true and a resolved secret; for now the only constructor is jiratr.New(secret, nil).

== Tests (internal/cmd/telegraph_test.go, NEW) ==
- Config-missing: temp town with no settings/telegraph.toml, runTelegraphStart returns wrapped not-found error.
- Validate-failure: telegraph.toml with enabled=true and no secret_env returns the validate error.
- Secret-missing: valid config but env var unset; returned error mentions both provider ID and env var name.
- Happy-path startup + signed POST round trip: lift the construction into an unexported runTelegraphStartImpl(ctx, deps) where deps carries a mail router seam + listener factory; start the server on :0, POST a signed Jira issue_created payload, assert mail lands in a memory router and Counters.Accept and Counters.Deliver each incremented exactly once.
- MUST NOT exec the gt binary (Makefile note: internal/cmd tests cannot run the built binary on macOS due to SIGKILL of unsigned binaries; mirror the testmain skip used in the existing cmd test suite).

== Other ==
- Existing internal/telegraph/... tests must remain green (go test ./internal/telegraph/...).
- go build ./... clean.
- Logs: cfg.LogFile when set, stderr otherwise.
- Config reload requires restart (per internal/telegraph/config.go:46). No hot reload in v1.

References:
- internal/telegraph/integration_test.go:78-123 (the canonical wiring)
- internal/telegraph/config.go (Load, Validate, ResolveProviders, ResolveSecret)
- internal/telegraph/transport/http.go (Handler.ServeHTTP)
- internal/telegraph/providers/jira/translator.go
- internal/telegraph/transform/mail.go (NewProduction)
- internal/telegraph/tlog/logger.go (Counters)
- internal/cmd/proxy_subcmds.go (cobra registration pattern)
- internal/cmd/boot.go (subcommand-tree pattern)

title: Telegraph v1 production rollout: gt telegraph start
description:
Wire the L1 (HTTP transport) → L2 (Jira translator) → L3 (mail transformer + nudge) pipeline into a runnable cobra subcommand and (optionally) into the gastown daemon, unblocking Jira webhook delivery into Telegraph at https://telegraph.phaware.care/webhook/jira via a Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel.

Operational driver: Cannot configure Jira webhooks at phaware.atlassian.net until Telegraph has a runnable entry point.

After this lands, the operator can:
  1. Drop telegraph.toml at ~/gt/settings/telegraph.toml
  2. Export GT_TELEGRAPH_JIRA_SECRET=<hex-32>
  3. Run gt telegraph start & (or under launchd / systemd later)
  4. Have a bound listener on :8765 ready for inbound webhooks

Library code is complete and unit-tested in internal/telegraph/{transport,providers/jira,transform,tlog,config,types}.go and integration_test.go:78-123. The gap: nothing in internal/cmd/, internal/daemon/, or cmd/gt/ constructs the pipeline. There is no gt telegraph cobra subcommand. The integration test is the only place L1+L2+L3 are wired together.

Phased rollout:
  Phase 1 (gt-…1) — gt telegraph start cobra subcommand + wiring + tests (foreground process model)
  Phase 2 (gt-…2) — Optional auto-start by gastown daemon (config-gated, restart-isolated from gastown as a whole)
  Phase 3 (gt-…3) — Operator runbook (nohup pattern + Cloudflare tunnel deployment)

Out of scope (defer): hot reload, additional providers, outbound writes, GET /healthz, launchd / systemd unit files.

References: docs/design/telegraph.md (epic gt-6if), internal/telegraph/integration_test.go:78-123, docs/design/refinery-pr-workflow.md §G21-G25.

PR #23 (test(telegraph): end-to-end integration test, gt-6ev) merged with 3 gemini-code-assist review comments unaddressed. Evaluate each and fix if legitimate.

**Comments (internal/telegraph/integration_test.go):**

1. **[HIGH] line 437 — TestIntegration_NoRawUserTextInSubject is ineffective**
   The injectionText is never actually included in the payload sent to the server. The test gives false sense of security re prompt-injection safety. Rewrite so the injection payload is actually sent end-to-end and the assertion runs on real Subject output.

2. **[MEDIUM] line 371 — Flaky time.Sleep for nudge**
   Transform calls maybeNudge after Send. Race: waitMessages returns but nudge hasn't fired. Replace sleep with a signal/channel or a poll-until-N-nudges helper.

3. **[MEDIUM] line 417 — Unnecessary time.Sleep after sync 401**
   post is synchronous; sig verification happens in handler before async work. If post returns 401, no async side effects are in flight. Remove the sleep.

**Acceptance:** Each fix is applied with tests continuing to pass. 'go test ./...' passes. Parent: gt-3k1 (closed epic).

title: Address skipped PR #16 review comments (Telegraph L3)
description:

attached_formula: mol-polecat-work

PR #16 (feat(telegraph): L3 mail envelope + rate-limited nudge, gt-ahf) merged with 4 gemini-code-assist review comments unaddressed. Evaluate each and fix if legitimate.

**Comments:**

1. **[HIGH/security-high] internal/telegraph/transform/mail.go:178 — Header Injection**
   buildHeaders() embeds NormalizedEvent fields (Actor, Subject, Labels) directly into headers. If any field contains a newline, it injects arbitrary headers (e.g., Bcc, Content-Type). Sanitize/reject newlines in any value that ends up in a header.

2. **[MEDIUM] internal/mail/memory_router.go:40 — Shallow copy in Messages()**
   Messages() returns a shallow slice copy, but Message has pointer/slice fields (CC []string). Caller mutation affects shared state. Deep-copy or return immutable wrapper.

3. **[MEDIUM] internal/telegraph/transform/mail.go:15 — Missing unicode/utf8 import**
   If we fix #4 via [\]rune truncation, need unicode/utf8 import. Add if needed.

4. **[MEDIUM] internal/telegraph/transform/mail.go:143 — safeTitle byte-truncation**
   safeTitle truncates by bytes (text[:maxTitleLen]); breaks multi-byte UTF-8. Switch to rune-based truncation.

**Acceptance:** Each fix has a test covering it. 'go test ./...' passes. Parent: gt-3k1 (closed epic).

Implement internal/telegraph/providers/jira/translator.go. Responsibilities: satisfy Translator interface — Authenticate() (HMAC via X-Hub-Signature sha256), Translate(body) → NormalizedEvent or ErrUnknownEventType. v1 event types: jira:issue_created, jira:issue_updated (status/priority/assignee), jira:comment_added, jira:comment_updated. Unknown types logged + rejected with HTTP 200 to prevent Jira retries. See design doc §'Jira Auth Scheme' and §'Jira v1 Scope'. Parent: gt-3k1.
close_reason: Telegraph L2 Jira translator merged to main (b3ba9b7) via PR #21

Implement internal/telegraph/transform/mail.go. Responsibilities: build Mayor-addressed mail envelope from NormalizedEvent (From/To/Subject/Body with Telegraph-* metadata headers and delimiters), send via gt mail, then conditionally gt nudge mayor/ with a rate-limit window (one nudge per window). Must land FIRST or alongside in-memory mail router (mail.NewMemoryRouter) so tests can run without hitting the production Dolt mail queue (PR #9 guard). See design doc §'Mail Envelope Contract' and §'Nudge Rate-Limit Window'. Parent: gt-3k1.
close_reason: Telegraph L3 merged to main (3ea5e10) via PR #16

Implement internal/telegraph/transport/http.go. Responsibilities: HTTP webhook listener (POST /webhook/<provider>), route by path/config to the matching provider Translator, delegate signature auth via translator.Authenticate(headers, body), enqueue RawEvent onto dispatch channel, respond with HTTP 200 on success and 4xx on auth/parse errors. Log accept/reject events. See design doc docs/design/telegraph.md §'Three-Layer Architecture' and §'Sequence: Jira Webhook → Mayor Mail'. Parent: gt-3k1 (epic).
close_reason: Telegraph L1 HTTP transport merged to main (ab593bd) via PR #22

title: town init / bootstrap: routes.jsonl should include deacon- prefix by default
description:
~/gt/.beads/routes.jsonl was missing the deacon- prefix route, which broke gt hook / gt sling resolution for any deacon-wisp-* bead. Result: deacon could create patrol wisps but could not hook them, manifesting as the recurring 'no issue found' / 'bd show failed' escalations (see closed hq-wisp-htu1d, hq-wisp-85hku, and the 2026-04-19/20 escalation cluster: hq-g8m, hq-yyz, hq-bzb, hq-nna).

Fix applied operationally: appended {\"prefix\":\"deacon-\",\"path\":\"deacon\"} to routes.jsonl on this town.

What this bead asks for: the source of routes.jsonl should be the cause. Either:
  (a) gt town init / gt deacon init should write a deacon- route by default, or
  (b) routes.jsonl should be auto-generated from gt rigs.json + a 'deacon' role marker, so adding any new agent (deacon, mayor, witness, refinery) automatically gets a route.

Acceptance:
- A fresh town created via gt town init has a routes.jsonl entry for deacon- out of the box.
- (Stretch) Same applies to other town-level agent prefixes if any exist.
- Add a doctor check (gt doctor or bd doctor) that flags missing routes for known agents present in rigs.json.

Repro steps (before fix):
  cd ~/gt/deacon
  bd show deacon-wisp-<id>          # works
  gt hook deacon-wisp-<id>           # fails: 'bead not found (bd show failed)'

After fix:
  cd ~/gt/deacon
  gt hook deacon-wisp-<id> --dry-run # 🪝 Hooking deacon-wisp-<id>...

References:
- internal/beads/routes.go:340 ResolveBeadsDirForID
- internal/beads/routes.go:252 ExtractPrefix
- internal/cmd/sling_helpers.go:44 resolveBeadDir

title: Telegraph: operator runbook + Cloudflare tunnel deployment notes
description:
Phase 3 of epic gt-mwy. Document the production deployment path so the operational driver (Jira webhooks at phaware.atlassian.net → telegraph.phaware.care/webhook/jira via Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel) can be executed.

== Deliverables ==
- docs/runbooks/telegraph.md (or similar) covering:
  - telegraph.toml example (production :8765 listen, jira provider enabled, secret_env=GT_TELEGRAPH_JIRA_SECRET, events list)
  - Generating and rotating the Jira HMAC secret (hex-32)
  - Foreground run pattern: nohup gt telegraph start > log 2>&1 &
  - Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel config from telegraph.phaware.care/webhook → http://127.0.0.1:8765/webhook
  - Jira webhook configuration at phaware.atlassian.net (URL, events, signature header)
  - Verifying receipt: tail of cfg.LogFile + signed test POST recipe
  - Restart procedure (Phase 2)
  - Where logs land and how to interpret tlog.Counters
- Cross-link from docs/design/telegraph.md.

Out of scope: launchd / systemd unit files (operator preference).

title: Telegraph: optional auto-start by gastown daemon (config-gated, restart-isolated)
description:
Phase 2 of epic gt-mwy. Once Phase 1 (gt-mwy.2) lands, integrate Telegraph into gastown's daemon so that 'gt up' (or equivalent) optionally launches Telegraph alongside the rest of the system.

== Acceptance ==
- Telegraph is optionally started by the gastown daemon when configured (e.g. cfg.Telegraph.Enabled or presence of telegraph.toml — pick the simplest signal that does not regress operators who don't run Telegraph).
- A failure to start Telegraph does NOT interrupt gastown as a whole. The failure is logged; other services come up normally.
- Telegraph can be restarted without restarting gastown as a whole.
- The daemon-managed mode reuses the same construction code as gt telegraph start (no duplicated wiring).

== Wiring notes ==
- Look at internal/daemon/dolt.go for the precedent of a daemon-managed external/internal service with restart logic.
- Likely surface: an internal/daemon/telegraph.go that supervises a Telegraph subprocess (cleanest restart isolation) OR an in-process goroutine with its own context cancel.
- Subprocess vs in-process: subprocess buys clean restart isolation matching the spec's 'failure does not interrupt gastown'; consider that first.
- Logs route to cfg.LogFile (already supported by Phase 1) so daemon-managed mode does not steal stderr.

== Tests ==
- Unit test that the daemon honours the 'Telegraph disabled / not configured' path and does not attempt to start it.
- Unit test that a Telegraph startup failure does not propagate to fail other services.
- Integration test (or test-double) for the restart-without-gastown-restart path.

Out of scope: launchd / systemd unit files (operator concern, covered by Phase 3 runbook).

PR #22 (feat(telegraph): L1 HTTP webhook transport, gt-d0t) merged with 4 gemini-code-assist review comments unaddressed. Evaluate each and fix if legitimate.

**Comments (internal/telegraph/transport/http.go):**

1. **[MEDIUM] line 14 — Missing imports**
   Need 'net' (for SplitHostPort, see #3) and 'os' (for os.Stderr fallback, see #2). Add when applying those fixes.

2. **[MEDIUM] line 38 — Nil logW panics in writeEntry**
   NewHandler docs say pass os.Stderr if logW is unset, but impl doesn't handle nil. Default logW to os.Stderr when nil.

3. **[MEDIUM] line 51 — RemoteAddr includes port**
   r.RemoteAddr is 'ip:port'. For source-IP field, use net.SplitHostPort to strip the port.

4. **[MEDIUM] line 71 — io.LimitReader silently truncates**
   io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(...)) silently truncates oversize bodies, leading to misleading HMAC-mismatch 401. Detect truncation explicitly and return 413 Payload Too Large.

**Acceptance:** Each fix has a test. 'go test ./...' passes. Parent: gt-3k1 (closed epic).

PR #21 (feat(telegraph): L2 Jira translator, gt-458) merged with 3 gemini-code-assist review comments unaddressed. Evaluate each and fix if legitimate.

**Comments (internal/telegraph/providers/jira/translator.go):**

1. **[MEDIUM] line 155 — CanonicalURL points to REST API**
   CanonicalURL is set from p.Issue.Self (REST API URL). Should be the user-facing browse URL (https://<domain>.atlassian.net/browse/<KEY>). Derive from issue key + instance base URL.

2. **[MEDIUM] line 173 — Empty changelogText leaves Text blank**
   If the update involves fields not in the changelog switch, changelogText returns empty and NormalizedEvent.Text is empty. Fall back to the issue summary (p.Issue.Fields.Summary) when empty.

3. **[MEDIUM] line 284 — Add 'summary' to tracked changelog fields**
   Switch statement tracks status/priority/assignee but not summary. Issue-title updates are not reflected. Add 'summary' to tracked fields.

**Acceptance:** Each fix has a test. 'go test ./...' passes. Parent: gt-3k1 (closed epic).

End-to-end test that boots the Telegraph daemon (L1 transport + L2 Jira translator + L3 mail/nudge) and POSTs a signed Jira webhook payload, asserts mail lands in the Mayor inbox with correct envelope metadata (Telegraph-* headers, From/To/Subject/Body), and that the rate-limited nudge fires exactly once per window across a burst. Uses in-memory mail router (NewMemoryRouter) to avoid production Dolt. Blocked on L1 (gt-d0t), L2 (gt-458), L3 (gt-ahf). Parent: gt-3k1.
close_reason: Telegraph integration test merged to main (ee3ad6f) via PR #23
